name: Mount Horeb
shortDescription: Mount Horeb, also known as the Mountain of God, is a significant location in the Hebrew Bible, primarily associated with the giving of the Ten Commandments to Moses.
description: Mount Horeb, often considered synonymous with Mount Sinai (though some scholars debate their precise relationship), holds profound significance in Jewish tradition. It is the location where God appeared to Moses in the burning bush, commissioning him to lead the Israelites out of slavery in Egypt. Most importantly, it is the place where God gave Moses the Ten Commandments, solidifying the covenant between God and the Israelites. The mountain represents a sacred space of divine revelation and law-giving, symbolizing the foundation of Jewish law and ethical principles. The narrative surrounding Mount Horeb involves themes of divine power, covenant, law, and the establishment of a unique relationship between God and the Jewish people. The events at Horeb are central to the story of the Exodus and the formation of the Israelite nation. It's a place of both fear and reverence, where humanity encounters the divine and receives guidance for righteous living.

location.cosmological: Located in the Sinai Peninsula, a desert region between Egypt and Canaan, marking a liminal space of transformation and covenant.

places.name: Red Sea
places.relationship: Part of the Exodus journey leading to Horeb

sources.title: The Pentateuch: A Feminist Companion to the Bible
sources.author: Athalya Brenner
